basicDetails.schemeName: Pre Matric Scholarship for SC students- DNH & DD

state.label: Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The scheme "Pre-Matric Scholarship for Scheduled Caste Students" launched by the Department of Social Justice & Empowerment, Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment, Government of India, aims to support the education of Scheduled Caste students studying at the pre-matric stage, reduce school drop-out rates, and encourage continuation of education up to higher levels. Through this scheme, annual scholarship assistance is provided to eligible Scheduled Caste students studying in Classes IX and X. The scheme is implemented by the UT Administration of Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu. The scholarship is provided as a consolidated academic allowance to support educational expenses. The scheme seeks to improve educational participation and progression of Scheduled Caste students to the post-matric level. > **Objectives** - To support parents of Scheduled Caste students for the education of their wards studying at the pre-matric stage. - To minimise school drop-out rates, especially during the transition from elementary to secondary education. - To improve students' educational performance and increase their chances of progressing to the post-matric stage.
schemeContent.benefits_md: 1. Financial support for SC students studying in Std. IX and X. 1. ₹3,500/- per annum assistance for day scholars. 1. ₹7,000/- per annum assistance for hostellers. 1. Additional 10% allowance for Divyang students. 1. Encourages continuation of education among economically weaker students.
schemeContent.briefDescription: The scheme aims to support the education of Scheduled Caste students studying in Classes IX and X. Through this scheme, annual scholarship assistance of up to ₹7,000/- is provided to eligible students.

children.text: The scheme "Pre-Matric Scholarship for Scheduled Caste Students" launched by the Department of Social Justice & Empowerment, Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment, Government of India, aims to support the education of Scheduled Caste students studying at the pre-matric stage, reduce school drop-out rates, and encourage continuation of education up to higher levels. Through this scheme, annual scholarship assistance is provided to eligible Scheduled Caste students studying in Classes IX and X. The scheme is implemented by the UT Administration of Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu. The scholarship is provided as a consolidated academic allowance to support educational expenses. The scheme seeks to improve educational participation and progression of Scheduled Caste students to the post-matric level.
Objectives
To support parents of Scheduled Caste students for the education of their wards studying at the pre-matric stage.
To minimise school drop-out rates, especially during the transition from elementary to secondary education.
To improve students' educational performance and increase their chances of progressing to the post-matric stage.
Financial support for SC students studying in Std. IX and X.
₹3,500/- per annum assistance for day scholars.
₹7,000/- per annum assistance for hostellers.
Additional 10% allowance for Divyang students.
Encourages continuation of education among economically weaker students.
Step 1:
Keep ready the soft copies of the required documents.
Go to http://www.scholarships.gov.in/. And click “New Registration”.
Guidelines for Registration will appear. Scroll to the bottom.
Read the undertaking carefully. Accept the Terms. Click “Continue”.
Step 2:
A Registration Form will appear. (The fields marked as * are compulsory)
Fill in the details and click “Register”.
Your Application ID and password will be displayed.
The same will also be sent as an SMS on your registered mobile number.
Step 3:
Go to https://scholarships.gov.in/fresh/newstdRegfrmInstruction
Click on “Login to Apply”. Enter your Application ID and password.
Type the Captcha and click “Login”.
On the next screen, provide the OTP received on your registered mobile number. You will be directed to the Password Reset screen.
Create a new password and confirm.
Click “Submit”. You will be directed to the “Applicant’s Dashboard”.
Step 4:
On the left pane, click “Application Form”. The fields marked as * are compulsory. Fill the details and upload the documents.
You can either click on “Save as Draft” to complete the application later.
Else, click “Final Submit” to submit the application.

applicationProcess.process_md: **Step 1:** Keep ready the soft copies of the required documents. Go to http://www.scholarships.gov.in/. And click “New Registration”. Guidelines for Registration will appear. Scroll to the bottom. Read the undertaking carefully. Accept the Terms. Click “Continue”. **Step 2:** A Registration Form will appear. (The fields marked as * are compulsory) Fill in the details and click “Register”. Your Application ID and password will be displayed. The same will also be sent as an SMS on your registered mobile number. **Step 3:** Go to https://scholarships.gov.in/fresh/newstdRegfrmInstruction Click on “Login to Apply”. Enter your Application ID and password. Type the Captcha and click “Login”. On the next screen, provide the OTP received on your registered mobile number. You will be directed to the Password Reset screen. Create a new password and confirm. Click “Submit”. You will be directed to the “Applicant’s Dashboard”. **Step 4:** On the left pane, click “Application Form”. The fields marked as * are compulsory. Fill the details and upload the documents. You can either click on “Save as Draft” to complete the application later. Else, click “Final Submit” to submit the application.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md: 1. The applicant should belong to the Union Territory of Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu. 1. The applicant should be studying in Class IX or Class X. 1. The applicant should belong to the Scheduled Caste category. 1. The applicant's parents' or guardian's annual income from all sources should not exceed ₹2,50,000/-. 1. The applicant should possess a UT domicile certificate. 1. The applicant must study in a recognised school with a valid UDISE code. 1. The applicant must be divyang students are eligible for an additional 10% allowance.

children.text: The applicant should belong to the Union Territory of Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu.
The applicant should be studying in Class IX or Class X.
The applicant should belong to the Scheduled Caste category.
The applicant's parents' or guardian's annual income from all sources should not exceed ₹2,50,000/-.
The applicant should possess a UT domicile certificate.
The applicant must study in a recognised school with a valid UDISE code.
The applicant must be divyang students are eligible for an additional 10% allowance.
